content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN non-negative integers.

In one move, you can do the following:


choose (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) such that 1ÔëĄLÔëĄRÔëĄÔłúAÔłú1 \le L \le R \le |A|1ÔëĄLÔëĄRÔëĄÔłúAÔłú.
Let X=MEXÔÇá([AL,AL+1,ÔÇŽ,AR])X = \text{MEX}^{\dagger}([A_L, A_{L + 1}, \ldots, A_R])X=MEXÔÇá([ALÔÇő,AL+1ÔÇő,ÔÇŽ,ARÔÇő]).
Insert XXX into the array AAA in any position (possibly at the start or end).
Given an integer KKK, find the minimum moves needed to insert KKK into the array. Note that you need to do this even if the array already contains KKK.

ÔÇáMEX(A)^{\dagger} \text{MEX}(A)ÔÇáMEX(A) denotes the smallest non-negative integer not present in AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,ÔÇŽ,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1ÔÇő,A2ÔÇő,ÔÇŽ,ANÔÇő.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum moves needed to insert KKK into the array.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2Ôőů1052 \cdot 10^52Ôőů105.
